markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Index rich document blocks, titles, links, tags, and embeds into Project Knowledge Index. This MT belongs to RichDocumentCore. Build the canonical structured document model backing the editor, Loom, UserManual, and projections.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Rich documents are PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT-backed structured block documents, not Markdown vault authority.
All rich document state must preserve Raw/Derived/Display separation and stable entity IDs.
scope.expected_behavior: DocumentSearchIndexBridge leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: DocumentSearchIndexBridge is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified (commits b506d832 backend core + 935e0482 manual + bf0c62a5 integration tests + dd3056f5 frontend); RichDocumentCore: 6 real-PG integration + 27 unit backend tests + 8 vitest (app suite 349/349). 18 block kinds, RawDerivedDisplay (CX-100), stable KBL- ids, save/load API w/ receipts, projections (negative proof: projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical), embeds (absolute paths DB-CHECK rejected), backlinks (stable KDLNK- relationship_id), history, permissions, crash recovery; migrations 0280-0282; RichDocumentView.tsx wired to real authority with stable selectors for MT-161+ GUI groups. UserManual coverage added (1 group + 10 cmd refs).

evidence.finding: Block/title/embed indexing into the Project Knowledge Index not implemented (links/tags only, as backlink edges); prior evidence overclaimed
evidence.fix: create/save/rename now index the document for real: first-class knowledge SOURCE row (kind rich_document, content-hash tracked, stale-marked on change, provenance-keyed KRD linkage) + first-class title ENTITY (kind rich_document, planner-confirmable per MT-130); links/tags remain backlink edges (MT-155), embeds in the typed side table (MT-152); block text is the source's content-hash-tracked indexing unit (span/passage extraction = index pipeline's job on stale sources)

findings.title: Block/title/embed content indexing into the Project Knowledge Index is not implemented
findings.evidence: The only persistence wired from documents is knowledge_document_backlinks (a link graph) + DocumentLinkReferences::tags() (backlink.rs:137-144). No code indexes block plain_text, titles, or embeds into the retrieval/search index. backlink.rs module header even says search-index bridge inputs - inputs only.
findings.fix: Wire document block text/title/tags/embeds into the Project Knowledge Index (knowledge_retrieval) on save, or descope MT-154 to link/tag edges only explicitly in the contract.

claim_mismatches.claim: Index rich document blocks, titles, links, tags, and embeds into Project Knowledge Index
claim_mismatches.reality: only links and tags are persisted, and only as backlink edges; blocks/titles/embeds are not indexed into any search/retrieval index

missing_tests: test: after saving a document, its block text is retrievable via the Project Knowledge Index

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
